command_spec._preamble: Exactly 5 public commands. Plain `hawking` dispatches to `status`. Six innate lifecycle flags recur across commands: --resume (re-enter a durable run at its last sealed checkpoint), --drain (graceful stop; release lease, seal, do NOT hard-kill), --verify (run the independent verification pass; read-only), --explain (compute + print the plan/decision WITHOUT performing side effects), --watch (follow live, re-render on change), --json (machine-readable, schema-tagged). A flag only appears on a command where it is meaningful; --resume/--drain are mutating and are FORBIDDEN on the read-only commands (status, doctor-default). Global: --profile <NAME> (lever bundle, existing main.rs:19) and clap --version/--help stay.

condense.authorization: Non-dry bake is owner-gated today (press bake prints a gated notice; bake-sidecar full bake is a follow-on). --explain is always allowed. Refuses if <bpw> is unreachable for the source without violating the byte-ledger.

command_specific.--memory-budget <SIZE>: Fit target (18gb|2tb|raw-bytes). Planner solves per-layer tiers to hit <bpw> under this. Was press --memory-budget.
command_specific.--profile <fast|race|efficient|exact>: Bake profile (predecoded scales aggressiveness). Was bake-sidecar --profile.
command_specific.--recover <off|qat|kd>: Post-quantization capability recovery (the QAT/KD 'doctor heal' sense). Default kd. This is the RECOVER half of 'condense = quantize+recover'.

lifecycle.--drain: Finish the current layer, seal a partial artifact, and stop.
lifecycle.--explain: Metadata-only Press Plan: what layers get which tier to hit <bpw> under the budget, projected size, WITHOUT baking. Replaces `press --dry-run`, `frontier-press-plan`, `bake-sidecar` plan-print.
lifecycle.--resume: Continue a partially-baked artifact from its last sealed layer/tile (bake is layer-checkpointed).
lifecycle.--verify: After (or instead of) baking, run the independent artifact verification: byte-ledger, tier-map coverage, decode parity vs source. Read-only when combined with an existing artifact.
lifecycle.--watch: Follow the bake (per-layer progress, running effective-bpw, quality proxy).

condense.handler: Python: tools/condense/second_light_pack.py + gravity_forge.py packers, eco_pipeline (Press->Summon), doctor_v5_gptoss_mxfp4.py staging. Rust plan/bake surface: crates/hawking/src/main.rs press_main (main.rs:1076) and bake_sidecar_main (main.rs:1027). Recovery (QAT/KD) is the proven doctor-recover path.
condense.json_output_summary: {schema:'hawking.condense.v1', model, target_bpw, achieved_bpw, plan{layers:[{tensor,tier,bits}],projected_bytes,budget_bytes}, recover_mode, artifact_path, byte_ledger_sha256, verify{parity,coverage}|null}
condense.one_line_help: Quantize+recover <model> to the EXACT target <bpw> and emit a served-ready artifact; obeys the bit-rate.
condense.state_ownership: OWNS produced artifacts: .tq / .hawking sidecars, their byte-ledger and provenance passport, and the Press Plan JSON. Does NOT own the gravity campaign lease.

command_specific.--bench [SUITE]: Run the measurement/benchmark suite (folds `bench`, `bench-q4k-shapes`, extracted bench-kernel/bench-server). Read-only measurement.
command_specific.--rank-profile <PATH>: Rank a recorded autotune kernel-profile via the offline scorer (was `profile-rank --profile-json`).
command_specific.--treat <off|qat|kd>: The ONLY mutating doctor mode: heal an EXISTING artifact in place via QAT/KD recovery. MUTATING + authorized; default off. (Distinct from condense --recover, which recovers while producing a NEW artifact.)

lifecycle.--explain: Explain the fit envelope / the diagnosis reasoning (per-Mac fit, KV budget math, why a rate collapsed) without heavy compute. Folds `fit`, `profile-rank` narration.
lifecycle.--verify: The INDEPENDENT verification pass (a second, non-producer code path): SHA-256 integrity + .hawking sidecar check, byte-ledger re-derivation, decode/tokenizer parity, shader/build fingerprint, readiness gates. Replaces `verify`, `spec-oracle`, `batch-hash`, `shader-hash`, second_light_precheck/readiness/gates, doctor_v5 verify/validate, eco passport.

doctor.handler: Rust: crates/hawking/src/main.rs doctor_main (main.rs:1054-fit_main, doctor_main:455), verify_main (main.rs:1044), profile_rank_main, stats_main, tokenize_main, bench dispatch, shader-hash inline (main.rs:1023), spec_oracle_main. Python: doctor_v5_gptoss_mxfp4.py (audit/verify/validate), second_light_precheck/readiness/gates/*_evidence.py, decode_parity_harness.py, size_frontier.py, codebase_census.py, eco_passport. --treat: the proven QAT/KD recover path.
doctor.json_output_summary: {schema:'hawking.doctor.v1', target, kind(source|artifact), size_bytes, sha256, fit{per_mac:[{mac,fits,intent,max_ctx}]}, kv_budget, rss, verify{sha_pass,ledger_pass,parity,gates:[{name,pass}]}|null, bench{suite,results}|null}
doctor.mutability: READ-ONLY by default. The single exception is --treat (authorized, mutates an existing artifact). Everything else, including --verify and --bench, only reads.
doctor.one_line_help: Inspect and (with --verify) independently prove a model or artifact; read-only by default.
doctor.state_ownership: Owns NO durable run state. May write only explicitly-requested report/evidence JSON to --out (e.g. sealed *_PROOF.json). Reads campaign/artifact state produced by gravity/condense; never mutates the lease.

args: <model>  (path to source weights: GGUF | MXFP4 STR2 dir | provenance manifest)
gravity.authorization: Requires the singleton campaign lease (succ_watchdog.SingletonLease). Ignition is owner-gated. --explain/--verify need no lease. No network, no multi-machine fan-out (removed).

command_specific.--max-experts <N>: Bound the per-block expert working set (live run uses 128). Was controller flag.
command_specific.--observe-seconds <N>: Post-ignite observation window before detaching (was ignite flag).
command_specific.--organs <LIST>: Restrict the tournament to named organs/blocks (folds gravity_forge_run --organs/--blocks/--experts, gptoss_gravity_run --blocks/--rates).
command_specific.--pq-max-rows <N>: Cap PQ program rows (was controller flag).
command_specific.--target-bpw <F>: OPTIONAL cap. Gravity chooses freely below this ceiling; omitted => unbounded search to the collapse floor. (Distinguishes gravity's CHOOSE semantics from condense's OBEY semantics.)

lifecycle.--drain: Signal the running campaign to finish the current row, seal, release the flock, and exit. Graceful; never corrupts the source (source is integrity-frozen after any terminal cell). Replaces `drain`/`stop`.
lifecycle.--explain: Diagnose-first: print the frontier plan, the candidate family tournament (PQ vs ternary vs RVQ+lowrank), and what row would run next, WITHOUT igniting. Replaces `gravity-explain-next`, `frontier`, `frontier-fit`, `second_light_ignite --dry-run`.
lifecycle.--resume: Re-enter the durable foundry campaign for <model> at its last sealed per-row checkpoint under the singleton flock. Idempotent; a second live invocation is refused by the lease, not by racing. Replaces controller `resume` and seed-c `resume`.
lifecycle.--verify: Read-only: re-derive the chosen family/rate from the sealed evidence + event-chain and re-check tournament receipts; prints PASS/FAIL without touching the lease.
lifecycle.--watch: Follow the live campaign (progress recounted from durable checkpoints, ETA, best candidate), re-rendering on heartbeat.

gravity.handler: Python owner: tools/condense/second_light_controller.py (SecondLightController.run/resume) driving gravity_forge.py (pack_transform_pq/pack_shared_grammar), second_light_pack.py (full-scope 128-expert grammar), gptoss_moe_runtime.py, doctor_v5_gptoss_mxfp4.py. Lease/heartbeat: succ_watchdog.py; events: succ_events.py. Native/engine core: crates/hawking-seed-c (Event Horizon / Gravity Forge, argv dispatch run|gravity-run|f2). The `gravity` verb is the single front door over BOTH.
gravity.json_output_summary: {schema:'hawking.gravity.v1', model, state(RUNNING|DRAINING|PAUSED|COMPLETE|FAILED|NOT_STARTED), lease{held,pid}, program_sha256, chosen{family,effective_bpw,rank,output_divergence}, frontier[], row{current,completed,total}, eta_s, best_candidate, artifact_path, event_head_hash}
gravity.mutability: MUTATING. Writes sealed checkpoints, event-chain, artifacts; holds the singleton fcntl lease. --explain/--verify/--watch/--json subsets are read-only.
gravity.one_line_help: Search for and forge the strongest sub-bit representation of <model>; the foundry CHOOSES the bit-rate.
gravity.state_ownership: OWNS the campaign root: GPT_OSS_120B_PQ_GRAVITY_PROGRAM.json (sealed program_sha256), per-row checkpoint.json, events.jsonl (hash-chained), the singleton lease/heartbeat, and the produced .tq/PQ artifacts. Sole writer of the lease.

serve.authorization: None beyond local bind. --verify gate is mandatory-by-default; disabling proof requires an explicit opt-out flag and is logged. Exactly one endpoint owner: refuses to start if the addr is already bound (no duplicate /v1 route, no second server binary).

command_specific.--auto | --intent <...>: Apple Fit auto serving config (existing serve flags).
command_specific.--autotune [--max-hours <F>]: Deterministically select+write a kernel-profile, then serve with it (folds `autotune`).
command_specific.--once [--prompt <STR>|--prompts-file <P: One-shot / batched generation to stdout instead of the HTTP loop (folds the entire `generate` command + batch-hash + batched-capture).

lifecycle.--drain: Stop accepting new requests, finish in-flight, then shut the single endpoint down cleanly.
lifecycle.--explain: Explain the chosen serving/performance config (kernels, batch policy, fit) without binding. Was --explain-performance.
lifecycle.--verify: The verify-before-load gate: run the artifact proof (byte-ledger, .tq strict/all-linear/gpu proof, sidecar) BEFORE binding the port or loading weights; refuse to serve on FAIL. Folds --tq-proof-mode/--tq-strict/--tq-require-all-linear/--tq-require-gpu into one gate that is ON by default and cannot be silently skipped.
lifecycle.--watch: Follow live serving telemetry (tok/s, batch occupancy, KV).

serve.handler: Rust: crates/hawking/src/main.rs serve dispatch (main.rs:694 -> hawking_serve::run) + generate_main (main.rs:860) + capture.rs (batched-capture) + autotune_main (main.rs:971) + batch_hash_main. The .tq proof gate is the crate `tq` feature surface, promoted to default-on for verify.
serve.json_output_summary: {schema:'hawking.serve.v1', artifact, addr, verify{passed,proof:{ledger,all_linear,gpu}}, config{workload,speculate,batch_policy,f16_kv}, fit{intent,max_ctx}, mode(server|once), generation|null, tok_s}
serve.mutability: MUTATING only in the operational sense (binds a port, holds a model in RAM, writes optional reports). Does not mutate artifacts or the campaign.
serve.one_line_help: Verify then serve <artifact> as an OpenAI-compatible endpoint (or one-shot generate).
serve.state_ownership: Owns the runtime server process + bound socket + in-RAM model + optional prefill cache. The SOLE OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Owns no durable producer state.

status.authorization: None. Must be safe to run against a live controller. Cannot import a code path that could start or drive the controller.

command_specific.--history: Print the hash-chained event log tail (read-only view of events.jsonl; folds `history`).
command_specific.--version: Include binary version + shader/build fingerprint (folds `version`, `shader-hash` identity).

lifecycle.--explain: Narrate what the running campaign is and what row/step it would do next (read from durable state, not by driving the controller). Folds `inspect`, `eta`, `gravity-explain-next` (read-only narration only).
lifecycle.--json: Emit the status object (the canonical machine surface; folds history/resources/version into one document).
lifecycle.--verify: Read-only integrity of the live view itself: flock liveness re-probed, heartbeat freshness, event-chain head hash re-verified, progress recounted from durable checkpoints. Never acquires-and-holds the lease.
lifecycle.--watch: Follow live truth, re-rendering on heartbeat/checkpoint change. Folds `watch`, `gravity-daily`.

status.handler: Python: tools/condense/second_light_status.py (already exactly this contract: reads state, never mutates, never holds the lease; STATUS_SCHEMA hawking.second_light.status.v1). Aggregates succ_watchdog.sample_resources, succ_events head, gravity chosen candidate. Rust `version` identity folds in.
status.mutability: STRICTLY READ-ONLY. No --resume, no --drain, cannot spawn/kill/lease the controller. Liveness is decided by probing the flock non-blocking and immediately releasing, never by holding it.
status.one_line_help: Read-only live truth for the machine and any running foundry campaign (this is what bare `hawking` runs).
status.state_ownership: Owns NOTHING durable. Pure reader of gravity's campaign root + host resources. Explicitly NOT the controller (the anti-shadow-controller command).

one_command_authority_plan.change_workflow: To add/rename/remove any surface: edit spec/hawking_cli.authority.json -> regenerate help/docs/completions -> run the authority tests. No hand-editing of clap, help text, docs, or completions is permitted (enforced by the drift gate).
one_command_authority_plan.drift_gate: A single CI check `cli-authority` fails the build if: (1) clap subcommands != spec; (2) any Python public verb is absent from the alias table; (3) generated help/docs/completions differ from committed; (4) any --json output violates its declared schema. authority.json is the ONLY place a human edits the surface.

generated_or_validated_artifacts.relationship: VALIDATED against authority.json by a build.rs/test that asserts the top-level subcommand set == the 5 and that every flag present in clap exists in the spec (and vice versa). Optionally a codegen step emits the Cmd enum + alias shim table from the spec so drift is impossible.

generated_or_validated_artifacts.artifact: Python CLI surface (tools/condense/*_cli.py, second_light_status.py, succ_cli.py, eco_cli.py)
generated_or_validated_artifacts.relationship: VALIDATED: a pytest loads authority.json and asserts each surviving Python entrypoint either (a) maps to a five-command target in the alias table, or (b) is an internal library with no public verb. The `status` reader is asserted to match the status command's json schema id.
GENERATED from authority.json (one-line help + flag docs come from the spec), so Rust --help and any Python argparse help read from the same strings.
GENERATED: the command reference and the deprecation table are rendered from authority.json + migration_ledger; a doc-drift test fails if regenerated output differs from committed docs.
GENERATED from the spec (clap can emit, but the alias shims are added from authority.json so completions know the deprecated spellings and their targets).
PARAMETERIZED by authority.json: INV-1 reads the expected 5, INV-7 iterates every alias row, INV-9 iterates every lifecycle flag. Adding a command/flag to the spec automatically extends the tests.
VALIDATED: each command's --json output is checked against the schema id declared in authority.json (jsonschema), keeping json_output_summary honest.

single_source_of_truth.format: One machine-readable JSON document: the 5 commands, their positional args, the innate lifecycle flags with per-command applicability, command-specific flags, each flag's {mutating, authorized, read_only, aliases[]}, the full alias/migration table (mirrors migration_ledger), and each command's declared json_output schema id.
single_source_of_truth.path: spec/hawking_cli.authority.json (+ a frozen sha in spec/hawking_cli.authority.sha256)
single_source_of_truth.why_json: Both the Rust binary and the Python tools/condense surface must consume the identical contract; JSON is the neutral interchange both parsers already use (STATUS_SCHEMA etc.).

status: SCAFFOLD - single source of truth for the 5-command surface; NOT yet wired to parsers (implementation deferred)
